{{Quote|It's ''Right to Rule''. One of the oldest in the fleet.|[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]]|<ref name="NJO III">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]''</ref>}}
'''''Right to Rule''''' was an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] that served as [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]] during the [[Imperial Remnant]]'s actions in the [[Yuuzhan Vong War|war]] with the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]<ref name="TCSWE" /> from [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]] to [[29 ABY/Legends|29 ABY]].

==History==
Following the tactical withdrawal from [[Yaga Minor/Legends|Yaga Minor]] to the planet [[Borosk/Legends|Borosk]] in [[28 ABY/Legends|28 ABY]], [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Pellaeon]]—having left [[Chimaera/Legends|''Chimaera'']] in repair at Yaga Minor—employed ''Right to Rule'' as his [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]]. The Star Destroyer would not only serve as the Fleet's flagship, but also as the flagship of [[Fleet Group Right to Rule|Fleet Group ''Right to Rule'']], one of four temporary battlegroups created for the defense of the planet.<ref name="NJO">''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]''</ref> During the [[Battle of Borosk (Yuuzhan Vong War)|battle]] that ensued, ''Right to Rule'' remained in the rear coordinating the movements of the other battlegroups, but would eventually be forced into the engagement when the starfleet withdrew below the minefield surrounding the planet. There it would remain to clean up the Yuuzhan Vong which had made it through the defenses.<ref name="NJO" /> One day after the Battle of Borosk, Pellaeon held a meeting with the [[Council of Moffs/Legends|Moff Council]], as well as numerous other high-ranking [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] officers, wherein he forced the Council to acquiescence to his intentions of allying with the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances]].<ref name="NJO" /> Following this meeting, ''Right to Rule'' led the Starfleet's Coreward offensive against the Yuuzhan Vong.

Pursuing Yuuzhan Vong forces under [[B'shith Vorrik]], ''Right to Rule'' led an Imperial contingent to the planet [[Generis/Legends|Generis]], but arrived too late to save the planet's long-range communications center. Determined to destroy the force, Grand Admiral Pellaeon continued to the pursuit, which led them to the Yuuzhan Vong attack on the [[Esfandia Long-Range Communications Base]] on the planet [[Esfandia/Legends|Esfandia]].<ref name="NJO III" /> Assisted by the Galactic Alliance frigate [[Pride of Selonia (Lancer-class)|''Pride of Selonia'']] and the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']], as well as the [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Relentless (Dorja)|''Relentless'']], ''Right to Rule'' acted as flagship in the [[Battle of Esfandia|battle that ensued]], forcibly placing itself between the Yuuzhan Vong and the planet in order to deter any further orbital bombardment and give time for the ''Falcon'' to evacuate the communication station's personnel.<ref name="NJO III" /> With the timely arrival of a fleet from the [[Ryn Network]], ''Right to Rule'' successfully led the forces at Esfandia to victory, utterly destroying the Yuuzhan Vong force, and killing Vorrik in the process.

''Right to Rule'' was next confirmed to have engaged the enemy at the [[Defense of Mon Calamari (Yuuzhan Vong War)|Defense of Mon Calamari]], acting as flagship for the Imperial contingent of the massive fleet gathered for [[Mon Calamari/Legends|the planet's]] defense. The fleet, and by extension ''Right to Rule'', operated subordinate to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Traest Kre'fey]] in a support role for the Galactic Alliance's [[Second Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Second]] and [[Third Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Third]] Fleets.<ref name="NJO TUF">''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''</ref> ''Right to Rule'' was subsequently assigned as the flagship of the coalition [[Fourth Fleet (Galactic Alliance)|Fourth Fleet]], carrying aboard it [[Vanguard Squadron (Hapes)|Vanguard Squadron]] and [[Twin Suns Squadron]], as well as controlling [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue]] and [[Wraith Squadron|Wraith]] Squadrons.<ref name="NJO TUF" /> In the initial forays of the [[Battle of Yuuzhan'tar|final battle]], ''Right to Rule'' assisted the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] squadrons assigned her in escorting [[Katarn Commandos|commando]]-laden troop transports through the [[dovin basal]] minefield surrounding Coruscant to descents planet-side.<ref name="NJO TUF" /> It would continue in the battle by leading the Fourth Fleet in engaging the Yuuzhan Vong home fleet protecting Coruscant and would end the war in that very place. Days after the official end of the war, ''Right to Rule'' was the place where Pellaeon returned ''[[Killik Twilight]]'' to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa Solo]] before returning to [[Imperial Space]].

==Behind the scenes==
As detailed in ''[[The New Jedi Order: The Final Prophecy]]'', Grand Admiral Pellaeon was a part of the fleet gathered for the [[Battle of Bilbringi (Yuuzhan Vong War)|failed attack]] on [[Bilbringi system/Legends|Bilbringi]], leading the contingent codenamed "[[Beta Fleet|Beta]]." It is very likely that ''Right to Rule'' was once again present as Pellaeon's flagship; however, the vessel is neither explicitly named nor alluded to.

==Appearances==
*''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic I: Remnant]]'' {{1st}}
*''[[The New Jedi Order: Force Heretic III: Reunion]]''
*''[[The New Jedi Order: The Unifying Force]]''
